/-- The sample space for an `n x n` Wigner matrix. It is the product of ‚ĄĚ over Index n. -/
abbrev WignerSpace (n : ‚Ąē) := Index n ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ

instance (n : ‚Ąē) : MeasurableSpace (WignerSpace n) := by
  infer_instance

/-- The measure distribution for a Wigner matrix. It assigns the measure `őĹ` to diagonal
entries and `őľ` to off-diagonal entries. -/
def wignerMeasureDist (n : ‚Ąē) : Index n ‚Üí Measure ‚ĄĚ :=
  fun (p : Index n) =>
    if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then őĹ
    else őľ


/-- The probability measure on the sample space `WignerSpace n`, which makes the
coordinate projections independent random variables with distributions given by
`wignerMeasureDist`. -/
noncomputable
def WignerMeasure (n : ‚Ąē) : Measure (WignerSpace n) :=
  Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist őľ őĹ n)

instance instIsProbabilityMeasure (n : ‚Ąē) : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure őľ őĹ n) := by
  rw [WignerMeasure]
  exact MeasureTheory.isProbabilityMeasure_pi (wignerMeasureDist őľ őĹ n) (by simp) (by simp) (by simp)

to the corresponding function (Fin n) ‚Üí (Fin n) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ that respects the symmetric structure of Wigner
matrices.-/

def wignerMatrixEntryFunction (ŌČ : WignerSpace n) : (Fin n) ‚Üí (Fin n) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ :=
  fun i j =>

    else if h_lt : i < j then

    else
      have h_gt : j < i := by
         apply lt_of_le_of_ne
         apply le_of_not_gt h_lt
         grind
      ŌČ (‚ü®(j, i), le_of_lt h_gt‚ü©)

@[ext]

    (k : ‚Ąē) (őľ őĹ : Measure ‚ĄĚ) (X : ő© ‚Üí Matrix (Fin k) (Fin k) ‚ĄĚ) (P : Measure ő©) where

  off_diag_dist : ‚ąÄ i j : (Fin k), i > j ‚Üí HasLaw (fun ŌČ ‚Ü¶ X ŌČ i j) őľ P

@[simp]
lemma is_measurable_Wigner (Y : isWignerMatrix n őľ őĹ X P) : Measurable X := by
  exact Y.meas

def Index (n : ‚Ąē) := { p : Fin n √ó Fin n // p.1 ‚Č§ p.2 }

instance : Fintype (Index n) := by

@[simp]
lemma off_diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n őľ őĹ X P) (i j : Fin n) (hij : i ‚Č† j) :
  HasLaw (fun ŌČ ‚Ü¶ X ŌČ i j) őľ P:= by
  -- Since $i \neq j$, we have either $i < j$ or $j < i$. We can handle these cases separately using the off_diag_dist and the symmetry of the Wigner matrix.
  by_cases h : j < i;
  ¬∑ -- Since $j < i$, we can apply the off_diag_dist condition directly.
    apply Y.off_diag_dist i j h
    all_goals unreachable!;
  ¬∑ -- Since $i < j$, we can apply the off_diag_dist hypothesis with $i$ and $j$ swapped.
    have h_swap : HasLaw (fun ŌČ ‚Ü¶ X ŌČ j i) őľ P := by
      cases lt_or_gt_of_ne hij <;> aesop
      generalize_proofs at *;
      have := Y.off_diag_dist j i h_1; aesop;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    -- Since $X$ is symmetric, we have $X i j = X j i$.
    have h_symm : ‚ąÄ ŌČ, X ŌČ i j = X ŌČ j i := by
      -- By the symmetry of the Wigner matrix, we have $X i j = X j i$ for all $i$ and $j$.
      intros ŌČ; exact Y.symm i j ŌČ
    generalize_proofs at *;
    aesop;

@[simp]
lemma indep_entries (Y : isWignerMatrix n őľ őĹ X P) (i j k l : Fin n) (hdiff : Sym2.mk (i,j) ‚Č† Sym2.mk (k,l)) :
    IndepFun (fun ŌČ ‚Ü¶ X ŌČ i j) (fun ŌČ ‚Ü¶ X ŌČ k l) P := by
  -- Define the projections from the Wigner matrix to its entries.
  let proj : {p : Fin n √ó Fin n // p.1 ‚Č§ p.2} ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ := fun p ‚Ü¶ (fun ŌČ ‚Ü¶ X ŌČ p.val.1 p.val.2);
  -- By definition of Y.indep, the projections proj are independent.
  have h_indep : iIndepFun (fun (p : {p : Fin n √ó Fin n // p.1 ‚Č§ p.2}) ‚Ü¶ proj p) P := by
    -- Apply the hypothesis that the projections are independent.
    apply Y.indep;
  -- By definition of proj, we have that (fun ŌČ => X ŌČ i j) = proj ‚ü®(i, j), by sorry‚ü© and (fun ŌČ => X ŌČ k l) = proj ‚ü®(k, l), by sorry‚ü©.

    exact min_le_max‚ü© := by
